title: [HandshakeToHW] Initial commit for handshake-to-hw
body:
As has been discussed for a while (#742), Handshake should eventually stop targeting FIRRTL and instead transition to the RTL dialects of CIRCT.

This initial commit handles the building of the top-level `hw.module` resulting from a `handshake.func` operation. All handshake operation modules are created as external hw modules. By doing so, follow-up commits may gradually implement each of the handshake operations using RTL dialect logic without requiring too many monolithic commits.

To transition from FIRRTL bundles with explicit ready/valid/data bundles, this commit relies on ESI channels for handshake module I/O as well as the top-level I/O. Being ESI based should hopefully also create a good basis for composing Handshake modules with other parts of future HLS and CIRCT infrastructure.

While this commit is largely a copy of that done in handshake-to-firrtl, one major change to the lowering style is no longer inlining the entire body of the `handshake.func` operation into the hw module. Instead, we maintain a value mapping from SSA values in the `handshake.func` to those in the `hw.module`, and maintain this using BackedgeBuilder operations.
